allowed_claim: AfriRide has a governed first-day execution script for collecting bounded pilot evidence.
authority_boundary: The day-one runbook coordinates controlled pilot execution only. It does not define replay truth, certify pilot completion, or authorize production readiness.
pilot_protocol_boundary: The live pilot protocol schedules evidence collection only. It does not define truth, certify production readiness, or claim completed deployment.

steps.action: Open pilot window and restate claim boundary to all roles.
steps.stop_check: stop if any participant treats runbook as production authority

steps.action: Register devices and capture signed device roster snapshot.
steps.stop_check: stop if any driver or rider device is unbound or unsigned

steps.action: Run preflight validators before any field movement begins.
Execute dry-run trip with stationary devices and replay the trace.
steps.stop_check: stop if replay readiness is not ready

steps.action: Run offline trip script with controlled disconnect and reconnect.
steps.stop_check: stop if buffered events cannot replay equivalently

steps.action: Run delayed sync script and replay late-arriving events.
steps.stop_check: stop if canonical replay changes final truth

steps.action: Collect GPS drift observations along the bounded route.
steps.stop_check: stop if GPS normalization changes pricing truth

steps.action: Submit duplicate signed event batches and verify idempotence.
steps.stop_check: stop if duplicate delivery causes duplicate execution

steps.action: Run dispute drill through replay authority with no manual override.
steps.stop_check: stop if support outcome diverges from replay authority

steps.action: Generate proof dashboard snapshot from collected pilot traces.
steps.stop_check: stop if dashboard reports drift or missing evidence

steps.action: Close pilot window and record no-claim escalation decision.
steps.stop_check: stop external reporting if any non-claim is violated
